Top 10 African countries the United States made the most money from last year - Business Insider Africa **In 2025, numerous African countries increased their trading links with the United States, exporting a sizable number of goods despite tariff conflicts, economic challenges,** **sanctions****, and shifting U.S.-Africa trade policy debates.**. * In 2025, many African countries strengthened trade with the United States despite ongoing tariff disputes and economic obstacles. * U.S. tariff changes and policy shifts created uncertainty for African exporters, affecting market access and contract negotiations. Overall, African exports to the United States remained important in 2025, with oil, precious metals, cocoa, cotton, and agricultural items taking the lead. Nigeria, Angola, Ghana, Côte d'Ivoire, and South Africa were among the top African exporters to the United States, demonstrating a wide export basket that includes more than just crude oil. Despite these, some African countries were able to export more goods to the United States than others. Ghana weighs local bids for $1 billion revival of Gold Fields' Damang Mine - GhanaWeb # Ghana weighs local bids for $1 billion revival of Gold Fields' Damang Mine. Ghana is seeking to boost local ownership in its mining sector. Ghana is assessing three bids from local investors to take over Gold Fields’ Damang gold mine, which requires up to $1 billion of investment to revive, the mining regulator told Reuters, nearly a year after the government seized control of the asset. Ghana, which is seeking to boost local ownership in its mining sector, rejected Johannesburg-based Gold Fields’ lease‑renewal bid and took control of Damang Mine in April, breaking with years of automatic extensions. Isaac Tandoh, acting head of the Minerals Commission, Ghana’s mining regulator, told Reuters on Monday that Gold Fields’ Damang lease, which expires April 18, would not be renewed. Ghana, Africa’s top gold producer, is key to Gold Fields, with output from Tarkwa and Damang underpinning its strong 2025 earnings.
